title: Handoff Artifacts
description: Typed handoff contracts between workflow stages. Each stage with a `produces` field writes .work-state/artifacts/<id>.json matching the schema below. Downstream stages declare these ids in `consumes` and read them from disk instead of receiving pasted prose. Artifacts survive context compaction and make the inter-stage contract explicit.

answers.description: User answers (interactive) or auto-resolved defaults with rationale (autonomous).

chosen.description: Option id chosen by user (interactive) or option #1 (autonomous).

api_contract.description: DTOs/endpoints shared by backend+frontend in full-stack tasks.

scope.description: Project-defined scope names touched (e.g. backend-kotlin, go, frontend, mobile, devops, rust). Matches scope_map in team.config.

verdict.description: Normalized overall verdict, derived DETERMINISTICALLY from findings (not eyeballed): `reject` if any unresolved CRITICAL finding; else `needs_changes` if any HIGH or MEDIUM; else `approve`. The review stage gate keys on this (`verdict != reject`) instead of a subjective 'confidence>=80'. An unknown/missing verdict blocks closure — it never auto-approves.

zone.description: Project-defined scope name (e.g. backend-kotlin, go, frontend, mobile, devops, rust) matching a scope_map entry. Used to route the Phase 6.5 fix to the right developer role.

findings.description: Only findings with confidence >= 80 are recorded.

manual_qa.description: Manual UI QA verdict, produced by the manual-qa agent AFTER review_fixes — it exercises the code that will actually ship, not a pre-fix snapshot. Sequenced (not run in parallel with code_review) for exactly that reason. Consumed by qa_tests (evidence → automated regression tests) and by summary. Replaces the old string field inside `debug`.

verdict.description: PASS only if every acceptance criterion was observed working. A missing/unknown verdict is treated as FAIL — it never auto-passes.

evidence.description: Concrete observations: screenshot path + WHAT IS VISIBLE on it, console/network state, exact steps performed. A bare 'looks good' is not evidence.

dod_additions.description: UI/visual acceptance criteria manual-qa appends back to dod.json (see multi-source DoD fan-in). Each item uses the dod item shape.

regressions.description: Pre-existing behavior that broke during testing — empty array if none.

qa_tests.description: Automated tests written by the qa agent AFTER manual_qa, encoding the manually-observed behavior (manual_qa.evidence) as durable regression tests. Only accepted when manual_qa.verdict == PASS, or when the task has no UI (!scope.has_ui) so manual_qa was skipped.

based_on_manual_qa.description: true when the tests encode manual_qa observations (has_ui path).

feature_spec.description: Optional slim feature specification — 8 sections distilled from a 15-section template. The discovery stage of a FEATURE workflow MAY produce it when the task warrants a written contract; it is not mandatory. Consumed by architecture and by manual-qa (acceptance_criteria becomes the manual test checklist). Gate-able via feature_spec_present when a project wants to require it.

goal.description: One-paragraph statement of what and why.

anti_scope.description: Explicitly out of scope — the boundary.

acceptance_criteria.description: Observable pass/fail criteria; manual-qa consumes these directly as its checklist.

testing_strategy.description: How the feature will be tested (manual + automated).

dod.description: Definition of Done — acceptance criteria fixed BEFORE code, each with how it is verified and (when closed) the proof. Produced early (clarify/discovery/diagnose), then FANNED IN by later stages: every role with relevant context may APPEND items (with source + unique id) or CLOSE an existing item (pending→met with evidence). The dod_complete gate and the Stop backstop (hooks/dod-gate.sh) require every item met WITH evidence before a task may claim done.

id.description: Unique item id, convention `<source>-<n>` (e.g. architecture-1). Lets fan-in contributors append without colliding and lets closers reference a specific item.

source.description: Stage id that contributed this item (e.g. exploration, architecture, code_review, qa_tests, manual_qa). Defaults to the producing stage.

criterion.description: Concrete, observable acceptance criterion — not 'works' but what specifically must happen.

verify_method.description: How it is checked: a manual-qa scenario, a screenshot that must show X, a test, a curl.

evidence.description: Proof: gist URL, screenshot path + WHAT IS VISIBLE on it (input/output present? errors?), test output, curl result, root-cause statement. A bare checkbox is not proof.

contributions.description: Optional audit trail of the fan-in: keyed by contributing stage id. The interpreter/hooks never require it, but it makes the multi-source history inspectable.

closed.description: Item ids this stage moved pending→met.

by.description: Agent name(s) that made the contribution.

updated_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the last append/close. Bumped on every fan-in write.

type_requirements_met.description: Confirms the per-type minimum items are present. Minimums: BUG_FIX → root cause named (not symptom) + repro-before reproduces + repro-after does not + affected scenario checked in manual-qa. FEATURE → each acceptance criterion mapped to a manual-qa step (both remote+docker modes if the feature touches agent/sessions). QA/report → published via publish-gist-report skill, screenshots actually visible (not broken). Any UI → DoD records what must be visible on the screenshot; on close, what is actually visible is written.
